name: Yeah Yeah Yeahs
description:
'''Yeah Yeah Yeahs''' (often abbreviated as '''YYYs''') are an American [[indie rock]] band formed in New York City in 2000 by [[Karen O]] (vocals), [[Nick Zinner]] (guitars, keyboards), and [[Brian Chase]] (drums, percussion). They rose to fame during the 2000s [[Garage rock revival|garage rock]] and [[post-punk revival]]s, noted for their evolving musical style and energetic performances.

After earning positive reviews for their 2001 [[Yeah Yeah Yeahs (EP)|debut EP]], Yeah Yeah Yeahs achieved commercial success with their acclaimed debut album, ''[[Fever to Tell]]'' (2003), placing them at the forefront of the 2000s rock revival. The album produced the single "[[Maps (Yeah Yeah Yeahs song)|Maps]]", which became their best-known song. Their next two albums, ''[[Show Your Bones]]'' (2006) and ''[[It's Blitz!]]'' (2009), drew further acclaim and produced the singles "[[Gold Lion]]" and "[[Heads Will Roll (song)|Heads Will Roll]]". Their fourth album, ''[[Mosquito (album)|Mosquito]]'' (2013), earned lukewarm reviews but reached number five on the US [[Billboard 200|''Billboard'' 200]]. After a hiatus, their fifth album, ''[[Cool It Down]]'' (2022), marked a critical resurgence and produced the single "[[Spitting Off the Edge of the World]]".

Yeah Yeah Yeahs have received [[List of awards and nominations received by Yeah Yeah Yeahs|several accolades]], including nominations for five [[Grammy Awards]]—four of which are for [[Grammy Award for Best Alternative Music Album|Best Alternative Music Album]]—and seven [[MTV Video Music Awards]]. ''[[Rolling Stone]]'' has ''Fever to Tell'' on their list of "[[Rolling Stone's 500 Greatest Albums of All Time|500 Greatest Albums of All Time]]" and "Maps" on their "[[Rolling Stone's 500 Greatest Songs of All Time|500 Greatest Songs of All Time]]"; the same publication ranked Karen O and Zinner among the greatest singers and guitarists in history, respectively.
